question.text: What is the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for handling stock exchange outages as prescribed by SEBI?

answer.text: The affected exchange must immediately inform SEBI and market participants; trading continues on unaffected exchanges; the affected exchange must try to restore operations and give 15 minutes notice before resuming
All exchanges must halt trading simultaneously if any one exchange faces an outage
There is no SOP — exchanges handle outages at their discretion without informing SEBI
Trading on all derivative segments is suspended for 24 hours when any exchange faces an outage

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI's SOP (Circular Jan 2023): (1) Affected exchange emails SEBI immediately, (2) Other exchanges continue trading normally, (3) Affected exchange attempts restoration, (4) 15-minute notice before resuming, (5) Extension of trading hours possible. Post-incident reports must be submitted to MIIs and SEBI. **The Trap:** Trading does NOT stop on all exchanges — only the affected one. There is a formal SOP, not discretionary handling. The 24-hour suspension is not mandated. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch8 covers this SOP. It applies to all exchange segments including derivatives. Series XIII tests understanding of market infrastructure resilience. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** During exchange outages, investors can use the IRRA platform to close positions on the affected exchange, and continue trading on other unaffected exchanges.
